title: Cell Hashing
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"9ge2f","text":"This protocol is for performing Cell Hashing only. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"e9lgp","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fhqsk","text":"Sample multiplexing and super-loading on single cell RNA-sequencing platforms.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"rfi","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2c0ct","text":"Cell Hashing uses a series of oligo-tagged antibodies against ubiquitously expressed surface proteins with different barcodes to uniquely label cells from distinct samples, which can be subsequently pooled in one scRNA-seq run. By sequencing these tags alongside the cellular transcriptome, we can assign each cell to its sample of origin, and robustly identify doublets originating from multiple samples.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":12}],"data":[]},{"key":"bj042","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8pcv5","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"41nij","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":[]},{"key":"2sqjg","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8dkei","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"62g4n","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"A0C6C83329EA4C799DF4F5FB834FB054","url":"https://www.biorxiv.org/content/early/2017/12/21/237693"}},"1":{"type":"imageblock","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"2BEBC3E356E742CBBA37574DCB16B152","height":"235","is_video":false,"mime":null,"original_name":"Screen Shot 2018-02-26 at 5.44.55 PM.png","placeholder":"https://content.protocols.io/tx2fw26.png","source":"https://content.protocols.io/tx2fw26.png","width":"578"}}}}

libraries:","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":34}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"88cjs","text":" ","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6ek7n","text":"We estimate that an average of 100 molecules of HTO per cell is sufficient to achieve useful information. The number of reads required to obtain 100 molecules depends on the complexity of the sequencing library (e.g. duplication rate). HTO and cDNA sequencing libraries can be pooled at desired proportions. To obtain sufficient read coverage for both libraries we typically sequence HTO libraries in 5-10% of a lane and cDNA library fraction at 90% of a lane (HiSeq2500 Rapid Run Mode Flowcell).","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"a9ffk","text":" ","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"c7io2","text":"","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ehvt3","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":3,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":[]},{"key":"baa3r","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bfp1l","text":" ","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"d261p","text":"Oligonucleotide sequences:","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":26}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4rpf2","text":" ","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cm9ec","text":"Hashtag oligos (HTOs):","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":22}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4sh4v","text":"These contain standard TruSeq DNA read 2 sequences and can be amplified using truncated versions of Illumina’s TruSeq DNA primer sets (see example D701_s below). steps: Obtain all single cell suspensions from different samples/conditions that will be multiplexed in the run. Keep samples in separate tubes until after cell hashing and shortly before loading cells into the single cell RNA-seq instrument. When aiming to super-load the same sample into one run, divide the sample up into equal proportions before staining with distinct cell hashing antibodies. Keep cell suspensions on ice (unless otherwise stated) at all times.

Carefully count all cells to ensure accurate quantitation. ● Make note of cell viability (>95%) and also include dead cells in the total cell count! ● If you observe many dead cells, live cell enrichment (e.g. by FACS) is recommended!
Resuspend ~1-2 million cells in 100 µl Staining buffer (2%BSA/0.02%Tween, PBS). undetermined, Staining buffer
Add 10 µl Fc Blocking reagent (FcX, BioLegend). undetermined, Fc Blocking reagent
Incubate for 10 minutes at 4˚C. Undetermined, Incubation 00:10:00, Incubation
Add 1 µg of single cell hashing antibody to cells. undetermined, Single cell hashing antibody
Incubate for 30 minutes at 4˚C. Undetermined, Incubation 00:30:00, Incubation
Wash cells with 1 mL Staining buffer (2%BSA/0.02%Tween, PBS). (1/3) undetermined, Staining buffer
Wash cells with 1 mL Staining buffer. (2/3) undetermined, Staining buffer
Wash cells with 1 mL Staining buffer. (3/3) undetermined, Staining buffer
Spin 5 minutes 400g at 4˚C. (1/3) Undetermined, Spinning 00:05:00, Spinning
Spin 5 minutes 400g at 4˚C. (2/3) Undetermined, Spinning 00:05:00, Spinning
Spin 5 minutes 400g at 4˚C. (3/3) Undetermined, Spinning 00:05:00, Spinning
Resuspend cells in PBS at appropriate concentration for downstream application. ​E.g. ​for 10x​ ~500 cells/µl; for Drop-seq​ [~200 cells/µl]; for super-loading​ ~1,500 cells/µl or higher.
Filter cells through 40 µm strainers (e.g. Flowmi cell strainer).
Verify cell concentration by counting on hemocytometer after filtration.
Pool all different samples/conditions at desired proportions and immediately proceed to next step.
Run ​Drop-seq (Macosko ​et al.​, 2015) or ​ 10x Genomics single cell 3’ v2 assay as described until before cDNA amplification.

Add “additive” primer to cDNA PCR to increase yield of HTO products: HTO PCR additive primer (2 µM): 1 µl (for 10x Genomics) or 0.4 µl (for Drop-seq) Subtract the total volume of additive primer from the water added to the PCR reaction.
Perform SPRI selection to separate mRNA-derived and antibody-oligo-derived cDNAs.
DO NOT DISCARD SUPERNATANT FROM 0.6X SPRI. THIS CONTAINS THE HASHTAGS.
Add 0.6X SPRI to cDNA reaction as described in 10x Genomics or Drop-seq protocol.
Incubate 5 minutes and place on magnet. 00:05:00, Incubation on magnet
Supernatant contains hashtags. Beads contain full length mRNA-derived cDNAs.
Proceed with standard 10x or Drop-seq protocol for cDNA sequencing library preparation. For hashtags <180bp (supernatant fraction), follow the sections below.
Purify Hashtags using two 2X SPRI purifications per manufacturer protocol. First, add 1.4X SPRI to supernatant to obtain a final SPRI volume of 2X SPRI.
Transfer entire volume into a low-bind 1.5 mL tube.
Incubate 10 minutes at room temperature. 00:10:00, Incubation
Place tube on magnet and wait ~2 minutes until solution is clear. 00:02:00, Magnet
Carefully remove and discard the supernatant.
Add 400 µl 80% Ethanol to the tube without disturbing the pellet and stand for 30 seconds (only one Ethanol wash). undetermined, 80% Ethanol 00:00:30, Ethanol wash
Carefully remove and discard the ethanol wash.
Centrifuge tube briefly and return it to magnet.
Remove and discard any remaining ethanol.
Resuspend in beads in 50 µl water. undetermined, Water
Perform another round of 2X SPRI purification by adding 100 µl SPRI reagent directly onto resuspended beads. undetermined, SPRI reagent
Mix by pipetting.
Incubate 10 minutes at room temperature. 00:10:00, Incubation
Place tube on magnet and wait ~2 minutes until solution is clear. 00:02:00, Magnet
Carefully remove and discard the supernatant.
Add 200 µl 80% Ethanol to the tube without disturbing the pellet and stand for 30 seconds (1 st Ethanol wash). undetermined, 80% Ethanol 00:00:30, 1. Ethanol wash
Carefully remove and discard the ethanol wash.
Add 200 µl 80% Ethanol to the tube without disturbing the pellet and stand for 30 seconds (2 nd Ethanol wash). undetermined, 80% Ethanol 00:00:30, 2. Ethanol wash
Carefully remove and discard the ethanol wash.
Centrifuge tube briefly and return it to magnet.
Remove and discard any remaining ethanol.
Allow the beads to air dry for 2 minutes (do not over dry beads). 00:02:00, Air drying
Resuspend beads in 45 µl water. undetermined, Water
Pipette mix vigorously and incubate at room temperature for 5 minutes. 00:05:00, Incubation
Place tube on magnet and transfer clear supernatant into two PCR tubes.

Prepare 100 µL PCR reaction with purified small fraction as follows: First, add 45 µl purified Hashtag fraction. %s %s Reagent Amount purified Hashtag fraction 45 µl 2x KAPA Hifi PCR Master Mix 55 µl TruSeq DNA D7xx_s primer (containing i7 index) 10 µM 2.5 µl P5 oligo at 10 µM depending on application* 2.5 µl * For Drop-seq use P5-SMART-PCR hybrid oligo. For 10x use SI PCR oligo. undetermined, Purified Hashtag fraction
Add 50 µl 2x KAPA Hifi PCR Master Mix. undetermined, 2x KAPA Hifi PCR Master Mix
Add 2.5 µl TruSeq DNA D7xx_s primer (containing i7 index) 10 µM. undetermined, TruSeq DNA D7xx_s primer (containing i7 index) 10 µM
Add 2.5 µl P5 oligo at 10 µM depending on application: ▪ For Drop-seq use P5-SMART-PCR hybrid oligo. ▪ For 10x use SI PCR oligo.
Cycling conditions: %s %s
Purify PCR product using 1.6X SPRI purification by adding 160 µl SPRI reagent. undetermined, SPRI reagent
Incubate 5 minutes at room temperature. 00:05:00, Incubation
Place tube on magnet and wait 1 minute until solution is clear. 00:01:00, Magnet
Carefully remove and discard the supernatant.
Add 200 µl 80% Ethanol to the tube without disturbing the pellet and stand for 30 seconds (1 st Ethanol wash). undetermined, 80% Ethanol 00:00:30, 1. Ethanol wash
Carefully remove and discard the ethanol wash.
Add 200 µl 80% Ethanol to the tube without disturbing the pellet and stand for 30 seconds (2 nd Ethanol wash). undetermined, 80% Ethanol 00:00:30, 2. Ethanol Wash
Carefully remove and discard the ethanol wash.
Centrifuge tube briefly and return it to magnet.
Remove and discard any remaining ethanol.
Allow the beads to air dry for 2 minutes. 00:02:00, Air drying
Resuspend beads in 20 µl water. undetermined, Water
Pipette mix vigorously and incubate at room temperature for 5 minutes. 00:05:00, Incubation
Place tube on magnet and transfer clear supernatant to PCR tube.
Hashtag libraries are now ready to be sequenced. Quantify library by standard methods (QuBit, BioAnalyzer, qPCR). Hashtag library will be around 180 bp (Figure 1).
